name: Chocolate and Chestnut Loaf
recipeInstructions:
1. Recommended to make 1 day ahead.
2. Melt the chocolate (at least 55% cocoa, up to 75% is great).
3. In a large bowl, beat butter until pale and creamy. Add sugar and beat until fluffy and light.
4. Add chestnut puree and beat until thoroughly blended and smooth.
5. Stir in melted chocolate, brandy, and 1 tbsp water until well combined.
6. Grease a 2 lb bread tin with oil and line with greaseproof paper. Brush lightly with oil on top of the paper.
7. Pour the batter into the tin and flatten the top. Cover with foil.
8. Refrigerate for at least 8 hours (or overnight).
9. Serve straight from the fridge, optionally decorated with piped cream and/or maron glace.
Note: This is a very rich dessert; one typically enjoys about three-quarters of an inch wide slice.
